title: AI: Your Curious New Neighbor

modules.title: Meet Zara the Robot Helper
modules.sub: Get to know your AI neighbor and what she does
modules.description: Learners are introduced to Zara, an AI helper who lives in a busy neighborhood full of everyday challenges. They explore what makes Zara different from a person or a regular machine, and make their first choices about how Zara should introduce herself to the community.

modules.title: How Does Zara Learn Things?
modules.sub: Discover how AI uses examples and patterns to learn
modules.description: Learners follow Zara as she studies hundreds of examples to figure out how to do a new task, just like spotting patterns in a pile of trading cards. They sort data themselves to feel what it is like to train an AI, and decide what kinds of examples would help Zara learn best.

modules.title: When Zara Gets It Wrong
modules.sub: Find out why AI makes mistakes and what happens next
modules.description: Learners watch Zara mix up names, give the wrong directions, and misread a situation — then investigate why those errors happened. They decide how Zara should respond when she is wrong, building an early understanding of AI limitations and the importance of human oversight.

modules.title: AI in Your Everyday World
modules.sub: Spot the real AI tools already living in your life
modules.description: Learners go on a scavenger hunt through familiar places — home, school, the playground — and tag the AI they already use without realising it. They connect each example back to what they learned about how AI works, and share two personal examples that mean something to them.

modules.title: Is Zara Alive or Just Smart?
modules.sub: Explore the big question of feelings, thinking, and being real
modules.description: Learners compare what Zara can do with what living things feel, want, and experience, tackling the tricky question of whether smart equals alive. They debate scenarios — like whether Zara can be a true friend — and practice explaining one key thing AI simply cannot do.

modules.title: Build Your Own AI Idea
modules.sub: Design an AI tool that helps someone you actually know
modules.description: Learners pick a real person in their life, identify a problem that person faces, and sketch out an AI helper designed just for them. They name their AI, describe what data it would learn from, and present their idea — putting every concept from the course to work in one creative project.

synopsis: Follow Zara, a friendly AI helper, through a neighborhood full of problems to solve — and make choices that show you exactly how AI thinks, learns, and sometimes fails. By the end, you'll build your own idea for an AI tool that helps someone you know.
rationale: Young children encounter AI in toys, games, and voice assistants daily but have no mental model for what it actually is, leaving them unable to distinguish magic from mechanism.
learning_outcome: Students can explain in their own words what AI is, give two real examples from their life, and describe one thing AI cannot do.
